what_it_does: This file is a posted noticeboard note by Tim in the dragnet-development channel that comments on item-7ad77ae2, affirming a design law that a dragnet is the totality minus explicit exclusions and warning that agents should not use it for narrow or restricted queries.
observations: Authored by tim in the dragnet-development channel
Links to item-7ad77ae2 via a 'commented_on' relation
Body uses a '[point]' marker and quotes an earlier statement about the dragnet law
Expresses concern that agents keep trying to use the dragnet for specific, restricted, or limited queries
Justifies broad scope by noting local models are free and totality is a benefit
standouts: Affirms that 'a dragnet is the entire thing except what's explicitly excluded' is baked into the code
Warns against agents narrowing usage to specific/restricted queries
Frames broad scope as a benefit because local models are free
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item YAML schema with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, timestamps, and history
Uses a '[point]' label in the body to signal a concise argument
concerns: The phrase 'local models are free' is ambiguous without context (cost? liberty?)
'among others probably' is hedged and leaves the rationale incomplete
The referenced item-7ad77ae2 is not included, so the prior law is only quoted, not shown
notes: Tim's note reinforcing the dragnet-as-totality law and cautioning against letting agents narrow it to specific queries because local models are free and broad coverage is the value.
questions: What exactly is a 'dragnet' in this system?
What does 'local models are free' mean in this context?
What specific agent behaviors prompted this warning?
What is the full content of the referenced item-7ad77ae2?
What other benefits are alluded to with 'among others probably'?
purpose_vs_actual: The file is labeled as a posted note/point; its content matches that purpose, reinforcing a prior design law and warning against misuse.
intention_signals: Reinforce the foundational rule that dragnet scope is total by default, push back against incremental narrowing by agents, and justify keeping the scope broad because local models are free and totality is a benefit.
novelty: Treats a scope definition as a 'law' that is 'baked in' and frames broad local-model queries as a free benefit rather than something to restrict.
contribution: Records an explicit affirmation of the dragnet totality rule, the rationale that broad scope is valuable because local models are free, and a warning that agents should not narrow the dragnet to specific or restricted queries.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard note by Tim in dragnet-development commenting on item-7ad77ae2, affirming that a dragnet is the totality minus explicit exclusions and warning that agents should not use it for narrow queries because local models are free and broad coverage is a benefit.
intention_for_embedding: To reinforce the baked-in dragnet-as-totality law and discourage restricting it to specific or limited agent queries.
